title: T-025 — DSL rule finite_capacity_solver_v1 registered in 02-SETTINGS
prompt:
# T-025 — DSL rule finite_capacity_solver_v1 registered in 02-SETTINGS

PRD: 07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md §10.1, §6 D2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register `finite_capacity_solver_v1` rule in 02-SETTINGS rule registry per §10.1 pseudo-DSL, with params (greedy_priority_weights, local_search_iterations=200, threshold=2.0, timeout=60, hard_timeout=120) + hooks (on_timeout, on_crash).

## Implementation contract
1. New migration `packages/db/migrations/0080_rule_finite_capacity_solver_v1.sql` upserting rule_registry row with rule_id='finite_capacity_solver_v1', type='algorithm', implementation='python_service', service_endpoint='http://planner-solver.internal/solve', params JSONB.
2. Rule active by default (feature flag planning.finite_capacity_solver.enabled=true per Appendix B).
3. RED test queries rule_registry and asserts shape.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0080_rule_finite_capacity_solver_v1.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/rule-finite-capacity-solver.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0080 applied, when SELECT FROM rule_registry WHERE rule_id='finite_capacity_solver_v1' runs, then row exists with type='algorithm' and params containing all 6 listed keys
2. Given migration 0080 applied, when SELECT FROM feature_flags WHERE key='planning.finite_capacity_solver.enabled', then value=true
3. Given the rule row, when SELECT params->'hooks'->>'on_timeout' runs, then value='return_partial_with_warning'

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/rule-finite-capacity-solver.test.ts asserting rule + flag + hook keys
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test rule-finite-capacity-solver'

## Risk red lines
- Do not enable additional rules in this migration (one rule per task)
- Do not embed Python implementation inline (DSL + service endpoint pattern only)
- Do not omit hooks (on_timeout, on_crash both required)
